message:
ci: remove dead cargo-llvm-cov install step (br-frankenpandas-0a83)

ci.yml lines 49-50 installed cargo-llvm-cov on every CI run
(~80s cold-cache + compile) but NOTHING in the workflow ever
invoked `cargo llvm-cov`. Pure wasted cycle time + false signal
to readers of ci.yml that coverage is measured when it is 0%.

Removed: the "Install cargo-llvm-cov" step. Nothing else touched.

Per br-0a83's "quick decision" recommendation:
  "if the team hasn't decided on coverage tracking yet, remove
   both dead steps today (2 lines) — that's the correct minimum
   move until a policy lands."

When coverage IS ready to be measured, the install step comes
back paired with `cargo llvm-cov --workspace --lcov --output-path
lcov.info` + Codecov upload + delta-gate in the same PR that
activates it — single coordinated commit rather than having the
install floating dead.

Cumulative CI-time savings: ~80s cold / 30s warm per run.
At swarm's 20+ PR/day velocity, ~30-60 min/week runner time
reclaimed for real work.

No source code changes. No test changes. Zero behavior change
except faster CI.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -46,9 +46,6 @@ jobs:
       - name: Install pinned pandas oracle dependency
         run: python -m pip install --upgrade pip -r crates/fp-conformance/oracle/requirements.txt
 
-      - name: Install cargo-llvm-cov
-        run: cargo install cargo-llvm-cov --locked
-
       - name: Format
         run: cargo fmt --check
